focus: Turn this contract into an implementation-ready delta list: pin the exact config/CLI plumbing for the opt-out flag, define the extractor input/output API around event-stream delta files plus template-faithful Note-column counts, and revise the phase-013 packet acceptance criteria/tasks so they point at the workflow-owned synthesis step and the existing synthesis docs/playbooks instead of reducer-local hooks and custom output columns.
noveltyJustification: 3 of 4 findings were newly pinned implementation deltas and 1 further refined the already-known delta/template contract into a concrete extractor API.
keyQuestions: Which failure modes and edge cases need explicit handling so the feature is idempotent, child-phase-safe, and resilient to malformed or partial evidence?
answeredQuestions: Which failure modes and edge cases need explicit handling so the feature is idempotent, child-phase-safe, and resilient to malformed or partial evidence?
ruledOut: Put the opt-out flag under config.executor or executor-config.ts
Use the packet's nested findings[] pseudo-schema as the extractor input
Keep custom Findings/Citations columns or special created_at headers

label: Add top-level resourceMap.emit plumbing with --no-resource-map mapped in command setup
summary: Both config templates lack a resourceMap block and the executor validator only covers config.executor.*, so the opt-out belongs in a top-level config section plus command flag mapping, not in executor-config.ts.

label: Extractor boundary should normalize delta JSONL files into template rows plus summary metadata
summary: The live contract is per-iteration JSONL event streams, and the phase-012 template only allows counts inside Summary or Note text, so the extractor should separate collection/normalization from markdown rendering instead of expecting nested findings arrays or custom columns.

label: Packet docs must be rewritten toward workflow-owned synthesis emission and existing synthesis docs
summary: The current phase-013 spec/tasks/checklist still target reducer-local wiring, custom columns, and new wildcard doc files, but the live synthesis feature and playbook surfaces already exist and should be updated in place.

Idempotence and child-phase safety require deterministic overwrite at the existing artifact_dir, use of the template Generated line instead of new headers, clean opt-out skips, and degradation only for malformed non-canonical rows while missing canonical delta artifacts remain workflow hard failures.

direction: Put opt-out under executor config or preserve packet-defined custom columns/headers
reason: Those approaches couple artifact policy to executor selection or fork the locked phase-012 template instead of reusing its Summary and Note surfaces.
